ls.lex_cards: [{"id":1784540671767,"name":"Landmark cases","ts":1784540671767,"cards":[{"f":"Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973)","b":"Propounded the Basic Structure doctrine — Parliament cannot amend the Constitution so as to destroy its basic structure.","known":false},{"f":"Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India (1978)","b":"Under Art.21, “procedure established by law” must be just, fair and reasonable; widened due process.","known":false},{"f":"Golaknath v. State of Punjab (1967)","b":"Held Parliament could not abridge fundamental rights by amendment (later modified by Kesavananda).","known":false},{"f":"Minerva Mills v. Union of India (1980)","b":"Struck down parts of the 42nd Amendment; the balance between FRs and DPSPs is part of the basic structure.","known":false},{"f":"S.R. Bommai v. Union of India (1994)","b":"Secularism is basic structure; laid down limits on Art.356 President’s Rule, subject to judicial review.","known":false},{"f":"Vishaka v. State of Rajasthan (1997)","b":"Laid down guidelines against sexual harassment at the workplace (later the POSH Act, 2013).","known":false},{"f":"K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017)","b":"Right to privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21.","known":false},{"f":"Mohori Bibee v. Dharmodas Ghose (1903)","b":"An agreement with a minor is void ab initio.","known":false},{"f":"Salomon v. Salomon & Co. Ltd. (1897)","b":"A company is a separate legal person, distinct from its members.","known":false},{"f":"Carlill v. Carbolic Smoke Ball Co. (1893)","b":"A general offer can be accepted by conduct; advertisement can amount to a unilateral contract.","known":false}]},{"id":1784540671768,"name":"Legal maxims","ts":1784540671767,"cards":[{"f":"Audi alteram partem","b":"“Hear the other side” — a rule of natural justice.","known":false},{"f":"Nemo judex in causa sua","b":"No one should be a judge in his own cause — rule against bias.","known":false},{"f":"Actus non facit reum nisi mens sit rea","b":"An act is not guilty unless the mind is also guilty (actus reus + mens rea).","known":false},{"f":"Ubi jus ibi remedium","b":"Where there is a right, there is a remedy.","known":false},{"f":"Res ipsa loquitur","b":"“The thing speaks for itself” — used in negligence.","known":false},{"f":"Ratio decidendi","b":"The legal principle/reason for a decision — the binding part of a judgment.","known":false},{"f":"Obiter dicta","b":"Remarks said by the way — persuasive, not binding.","known":false},{"f":"Stare decisis","b":"To stand by decided cases — the doctrine of precedent.","known":false},{"f":"Ignorantia juris non excusat","b":"Ignorance of the law is no excuse.","known":false},{"f":"Volenti non fit injuria","b":"To a willing person no injury is done — a defence in tort.","known":false},{"f":"Caveat emptor","b":"Let the buyer beware.","known":false},{"f":"Ejusdem generis","b":"“Of the same kind” — a rule of statutory interpretation.","known":false}]},{"id":1784540671769,"name":"Constitutional GK","ts":1784540671767,"cards":[{"f":"Which Article is the “heart and soul” of the Constitution?","b":"Article 32 — right to constitutional remedies (Dr B.R. Ambedkar).","known":false},{"f":"How many Fundamental Duties are there now?","b":"Eleven (10 by the 42nd Amendment + 1 by the 86th).","known":false},{"f":"Part IV of the Constitution deals with?","b":"Directive Principles of State Policy (Arts. 36–51).","known":false},{"f":"Minimum age to be President of India?","b":"35 years (Article 58).","known":false},{"f":"Which Schedule contains the anti-defection law?","b":"The Tenth Schedule (52nd Amendment, 1985).","known":false},{"f":"Retirement age of a High Court judge?","b":"62 years (a Supreme Court judge: 65).","known":false},{"f":"Which Article abolishes untouchability?","b":"Article 17.","known":false},{"f":"Writ to produce a detained person before court?","b":"Habeas corpus.","known":false}]}]
